title: Is the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_89 × Z_25) prime?
generalization_signal: The feeder suggests a new large-p regime on the four-class Γ(Z_p × Z_25) line: instead of absorbing barrier multiples into A, choose the three nontrivial B labels above half the interval so B has no spill, fix A inside a tiny 2,3-smooth reservoir, and fill C from the much larger complementary set of labels coprime to 6 and to the high barrier.
strongest_honest_claim: An explicit prime-labeling witness for Γ(Z_89 × Z_25) survives skeptical verification. This does not prove the family theorem, but it extends the live high-barrier complementary-support evidence beyond the previously preserved feeder range through z83 and supports the campaign claim that a spill-free high barrier on B together with a complementary C pool can handle larger p on the four-class Γ(Z_p × Z_25) line.
candidate_theorem_slice: A high-barrier complementary-support sufficient-condition theorem for Γ(Z_p × Z_25): if N = 5p + 19 admits three pairwise-coprime labels q1,q2,q3 in (N/2, N] and at least p - 1 labels in {1,...,N} coprime to 6q1q2q3, then taking A from 20 fixed 2,3-smooth labels, B = {1,q1,q2,q3}, C from that complementary coprime pool, and D as the remaining complement yields a prime labeling.
next_action: promote the verified p = 89 high-barrier feeder into the family wrapper dossier, then run generalize or publication_audit on the quantified complementary-support C-supply corollary before testing z97-z25; do not escalate this instance alone to Lean yet
